text: We present DeepPose, a deep neural network approach to the problem of human pose estimation, which seeks to predict the location of body joints by utilizing the expressiveness of deep learning. In stark contrast to existing part-based models that use a variety of techniques such as local pose classifiers and constrained possible body layouts, we formulate pose estimation as a purely regression problem. Our approach trains a multi-layered convolutional neural network (CNN) to discover and model the non-linear body of interactions between joints given an input image. In order to obtain the highest possible pose estimation accuracy, we develop a cascade strategy for a series of deep neural networks, which not only trains the network to find an initial coarse set of pose estimations, but also refines subsequent models to make predictions for smaller, higher resolution, patches about the estimated joints of the human body. Finally, our experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method for estimating human poses. We outperform existing state-of-the-art results by a large margin on three very diverse datasets that exhibit large degrees of flexibility in human pose configurations and numerous ambiguities regarding body parts, as well as large amounts of occlusions and variations in appearances of humans. Moreover, our fast testing time is extremely beneficial for real time pose estimation applications.
